topic.what: Comprehensive survey of split learning integration with LLM fine-tuning
topic.how: Proposes unified training pipeline taxonomy across model-level, system-level, and privacy dimensions
topic.why_matters: Addresses dual bottlenecks of computational resource constraints and data privacy that prevent resource-constrained organizations from adapting billion-parameter LLMs

characteristics.what_it_measures: Volume of intermediate activations and gradients exchanged per training step
characteristics.evidence: Survey discusses massive intermediate activations for LLMs requiring compression strategies
characteristics.context: LLM activations are orders of magnitude larger than traditional CV/NLP tasks

characteristics.what_it_measures: Global model accuracy under heterogeneous client data distributions
characteristics.evidence: Methods like FlexP-SFL, CSFL, GSFL report improved convergence vs baseline under Non-IID conditions
characteristics.context: Multiple methods validated on CIFAR partitions; LLM-native validation limited

characteristics.what_it_measures: Effectiveness of reconstruction attacks on smashed data and gradients
characteristics.evidence: Survey categorizes smashed data inversion and gradient-based label reconstruction attacks with varying success rates
characteristics.context: Attack methods discussed with defense strategies achieving varying protection levels

characteristics.what_it_measures: Balance between privacy protection and downstream model utility
characteristics.evidence: DP-based methods trade 5-15% accuracy for provable privacy; learning-based methods like NoPeek preserve more utility
characteristics.context: Perturbation methods sacrifice more utility than learning-based approaches

requires: communication channel between clients and server
server-side GPU resources for trunk model

novelty: First comprehensive survey exclusively on split learning for LLM fine-tuning
Unified fine-grained training pipeline decomposition (11-step workflow)
Component-level bottleneck mapping across model, system, and privacy dimensions
Three-part taxonomy anchoring literature to specific execution steps
open_problems: Native LLM empirical validation of most methods remains limited
Homomorphic encryption impractical for LLM-scale activations
Optimal split point selection for specific LLM architectures unaddressed
Long-term privacy guarantees under iterative training rounds unclear
